verdict_reason: The engineering layer carefully tracks the four-tradition reading of kauśalam: Shankara's 'skilfulness as wisdom-of-equanimity-during-action', Ramanuja's 'evenness possible through great skill', Prabhupada's 'art of all work' (in Kṛṣṇa consciousness), Mukundananda's 'art of working skillfully (in proper consciousness)'. The engineering body simplifies toward the Shankara/Ramanuja convergence — kauśalam = skill of acting from samatvam — which is the doctrinal floor common to all four. This is honest simplification, not tradition-conflation. The verse's harder claim (BOTH sukṛta and duṣkṛte are shed) is preserved without forcing a particular tradition's reading of HOW that happens (Shankara: through purification + Knowledge; Ramanuja: through devotional renunciation; Gaudiya: through Kṛṣṇa-consciousness). The 'sheds both good and bad' is rendered structurally. Sanskrit terms — kauśalam, samatvam, buddhi-yukta, sukṛta-duṣkṛte — are preserved. Pass.
